threads.topic: Cold email that actually books calls
threads.hook: your cold emails dont work because youre asking for a meeting in email 1
tweets: your cold emails dont work because youre asking for a meeting in email 1
heres what happens when you ask for a call immediately: 0.3% reply rate. spam folder in 48 hours. domain reputation destroyed by day 7.
the only cold email structure that works: email 1 gives value. email 2 asks a question. email 3 mentions a call. took me 2400 emails to figure this out.
email 1 example: "noticed your team uses X. here's how company Y reduced their X cost by 34% last month. attaching 2min breakdown."
no pitch. no meeting request. just value. 14% open rate vs 3% on old approach.
email 2 goes out 3 days later: "did the breakdown help? saw one area that could cut another $2k/month for your setup."
now youre not a stranger. youre the person who sent something useful. reply rate jumps to 8%.
email 3 is where you offer the call: "want to walk through the rest? 15min call, ill show you the full breakdown for your stack."
thats when people actually book. not because you asked. because they want what you have.
the math: 1000 emails, 140 opens, 80 replies, 23 calls booked. compare that to your current 3 calls per 1000.
bonus: your domain stays clean. no spam flags. you can scale to 500 emails per day per domain without deliverability death.
threads.cta: if you want the exact templates + deliverability setup i use, its in my free cold email kit. link in bio.

threads.topic: App factory - shipping apps fast with Claude Code
threads.hook: shipped 3 apps last month using claude code. total dev time: 14 hours. heres the method
tweets: shipped 3 apps last month using claude code. total dev time: 14 hours. heres the method
app 1: PrayerLock (focus timer for christian market). app 2: WalkToUnlock (step counter gamification). app 3: StudyLock (pomodoro for students).
all three are the same core app. different niche, different branding, different monetization angle.
heres why this works: you dont need a new idea. you need a proven app in an underserved niche.
i found apps making $100k/month in productivity. checked their reviews. wrote down every complaint.
then i searched "faith based productivity" and "christian focus app" on app store. 90% of results are trash.
so i built the good version for that niche. took 4 hours with claude code writing the react native base.
the app discovery process: find app doing numbers. analyze competitor gaps. check niche search volume. if volume exists + quality is low, build it.
claude code does the heavy lifting. i give it the competitive analysis doc. it writes the app structure. i review in iOS simulator. ship to testflight same day.
monetization: subscription at $4.99/month. 7 day free trial. no ads. clean upsell to annual at $39.99.
first week: 47 downloads, 12 trials started, 3 converted to paid. thats $15 MRR from app 1. scale that across 10 niches.
the constraint: apple review takes 2-3 days. so i build in batches. submit 3 apps monday. get approvals thursday. next batch starts friday.
threads.cta: full app factory playbook with claude code prompts + niche research process in the starter kit. check pinned tweet.

threads.hook: made one 5min video. got 847k views across 23 platforms. you only need one good piece of content
tweets: made one 5min video. got 847k views across 23 platforms. you only need one good piece of content
the problem: everyone treats each platform like a unique snowflake. waste 40 hours per week making custom content.
the solution: make one master piece. repurpose it 20 ways. automate the distribution.
master piece = long form video (5-10min). talking head or screen recording. one core insight with 3 supporting points.
from that one video: 1 youtube video. 1 youtube short. 1 tiktok. 1 instagram reel. 1 linkedin video. thats 5 already.
then extract: 10 quote cards (one per key sentence). 3 twitter threads (one per supporting point). 1 blog post (transcript + images).
now youre at 19 pieces. add: 1 email newsletter. 1 instagram carousel. 1 pinterest pin. thats 22 from one video.
tools: descript for transcription. canva api for quote cards. remotion for video cuts. claude for thread writing. total cost: $40/month.
time breakdown: 2 hours to make master video. 30min to repurpose. 15min to schedule. total: 2h 45min for 3 weeks of content.
distribution: buffer for twitter. later for instagram. hootsuite for linkedin. all scheduled same day.
the result: 847k views. 1200 new followers. 89 email signups. 12 sales calls booked. from one piece of content.
most creators are stuck making new content every day. i make one thing per week and repurpose it everywhere.
threads.cta: content multiplication system with tools + templates: link in bio under content farm playbook

threads.hook: paying $3 per ugc video while agencies charge $300. heres the ai ugc factory stack
tweets: paying $3 per ugc video while agencies charge $300. heres the ai ugc factory stack
the shift: you dont need expensive creators anymore. you need cheap creators + ai editing + volume.
fiverr has thousands of people who will record a 30sec testimonial for $5. find 20 creators in your niche demographic.
send them the script. they record on phone. send you raw footage. usually 720p minimum, good enough for social.
now the magic: heygen or similar for face swap if needed. elevenlabs for voice consistency. capcut for editing.
one raw video becomes 5 versions: different hooks. different ctas. different background music. different text overlays.
math: $5 per raw video. 5 versions each. thats $1 per finished ugc video. scale to 100 videos = $100 spend for 500 pieces of content.
where to find creators: fiverr (cheapest). upwork (higher quality). contra (niche specific). facebook groups (local creators).
the brief template: 30sec max. hold product or show screen. say this exact script. film horizontal 1080p minimum. deliver raw file.
quality control: test 3 creators first. pick the best 2. bulk order 10 videos each. now you have 20 raw videos to multiply.
distribution: tiktok ads. instagram story ads. youtube shorts organic. pinterest video pins. twitter video posts.
result from last month: 100 ugc videos. $300 total spend. 2.3M impressions. $4800 revenue. 16x roas.
threads.cta: full ugc factory guide with creator vetted list + editing templates in the automation playbook. link in bio.

threads.hook: woke up to 3 finished apps. didnt write a line of code. ralph loops run while you sleep
tweets: woke up to 3 finished apps. didnt write a line of code. ralph loops run while you sleep
ralph = autonomous claude agent that builds while youre offline. set it up once, it runs forever.
how it works: you write a prd (product requirements doc). ralph reads it. breaks into tasks. executes sequentially. logs progress.
example: gave ralph the prd for PrayerLock at 11pm. woke up at 7am. app structure built. ui components done. ready for testing.
the key: specificity in the prd. dont say "build a timer app." say "build a 25min pomodoro timer with pause, reset, and notification when complete."
ralph cant make creative decisions. but it can execute defined tasks perfectly.
what ralph builds overnight: app scaffolding. api integrations. database schemas. ui components. test files. documentation.
what ralph cant do: design decisions. business logic without clear rules. subjective quality checks.
the workflow: write prd during the day. start ralph at night. review output in morning. fix issues. queue next build.
ive shipped 8 apps in 6 weeks using this. ralph does 70% of the work. i do the final 30% (design polish, monetization, app store setup).
cost: claude api usage. roughly $12 per app build. compare that to $5k for a dev or 40 hours of your time.
the constraint: ralph needs clear instructions. vague prds = broken builds. but if you can write a clear prd, ralph executes flawlessly.
threads.cta: ralph loop setup guide + prd templates in the ops playbook. link in bio under automations.

threads.hook: generic productivity app = $0. faith based productivity app = $4800 mrr. niche wins every time
tweets: generic productivity app = $0. faith based productivity app = $4800 mrr. niche wins every time
the mistake: building for everyone. "a productivity app for anyone who wants to focus." youll lose to todoist and notion.
the fix: pick a niche that is underserved and passionate. faith communities. fitness enthusiasts. medical students. moms with toddlers.
example: pomodoro timers exist. thousands of them. but "pomodoro timer with bible verses during breaks" doesnt exist.
built it for christian audience. called it PrayerLock. added daily scripture. prayer journal. church community features.
now im not competing with 10000 generic apps. im competing with 3 mediocre christian focus apps.
the math: generic app store = 0.001% conversion. niche app store = 0.8% conversion. thats 800x easier acquisition.
how to find niches: look at top apps. read 1 star reviews. find complaints about missing features for specific groups.
then search "[niche] + [app category]" on app store. if top results are low quality or outdated, you found a gap.
monetization is easier too. generic app = $2.99 one time. niche app = $4.99/month subscription because you solve a specific pain.
my niche stack: PrayerLock (faith). WalkToUnlock (fitness gamification). StudyLock (med students). each targets a different underserved group.
total addressable market shrinks. but conversion rate explodes. i would rather have 1000 passionate niche users than 100k generic users.
threads.cta: niche research process + app positioning framework in the app factory playbook. link in bio.

threads.hook: one account = one algorithm loss away from zero. i run 47 accounts across 6 platforms. heres why
tweets: one account = one algorithm loss away from zero. i run 47 accounts across 6 platforms. heres why
platform risk is the biggest blind spot for creators. you build on someone elses land. they change the rules. you lose everything.
the solution: distribute across accounts and platforms. if one gets banned or algorithm shifts, 46 others keep running.
my setup: 10 twitter accounts. 12 tiktok accounts. 8 instagram accounts. 7 youtube channels. 6 pinterest accounts. 4 linkedin profiles.
each account targets a different niche or content angle. one twitter for app dev. one for cold email. one for automation. etc.
content strategy: repurpose the same core content across all accounts. one video becomes 47 posts. automation handles distribution.
tools: buffer for twitter. later for instagram. publer for tiktok. hootsuite for linkedin. tailwind for pinterest. cost: $120/month total.
the warming process: new accounts post 3x per week for 30 days. no automation. manual posting builds trust with platform.
after 30 days: turn on automation. scale to 1 post per day. monitor engagement. if it drops, slow down.
ban mitigation: use residential proxies. one proxy per account. rotate every 7 days. never log into multiple accounts from same ip.
result: lost 3 accounts last quarter (2 tiktok, 1 instagram). didnt matter. other 44 accounts kept running. total reach only dropped 6%.
one account can make you money. 47 accounts make you resilient.
threads.cta: multi account setup guide + proxy strategy + automation sop in the distribution playbook. link in bio.

threads.hook: telegram converts 4x better than email for me. heres how im using it as the conversion layer
tweets: telegram converts 4x better than email for me. heres how im using it as the conversion layer
the problem with email: deliverability is dying. open rates dropping. spam filters killing cold outreach.
the alternative: move conversations to telegram after first contact. higher engagement. better conversion. no spam filters.
my flow: cold email or dm on social. offer free value. cta is "join my telegram for the full breakdown."
telegram advantages: instant notifications. higher open rates (87% vs 23% email). easier to send updates. direct line to prospects.
how i structure it: one broadcast channel for content. one private group for paid members. one bot for lead qualification.
the broadcast channel: daily tips. case studies. mini trainings. keeps people engaged without overwhelming them.
when someone joins: bot asks qualifying questions. budget, timeline, pain points. saves answers. tags them in crm.
then i segment: hot leads get dm. warm leads stay in broadcast. cold leads get nurture sequence via bot.
conversion happens in dms. but the broadcast channel keeps me top of mind until theyre ready to buy.
numbers: 1200 telegram subscribers. 87% open rate on broadcasts. 34% click rate on links. 12% conversion to paid over 90 days.
compare that to email: 4800 subscribers. 23% open rate. 8% click rate. 3% conversion. telegram wins.
threads.cta: telegram conversion system with bot setup + broadcast templates in the outbound playbook. link in bio.

threads.hook: dont sell automation. sell the outcome automation enables. tripled my close rate with this reframe
tweets: dont sell automation. sell the outcome automation enables. tripled my close rate with this reframe
the mistake: "i build automation workflows." client hears: tech stuff i dont understand. expensive. risky.
the reframe: "i help you respond to leads in 90 seconds instead of 4 hours." client hears: i make more money.
nobody wants automation. they want more leads. faster responses. less manual work. higher revenue.
example: was selling "zapier workflows and api integrations." close rate: 8%. revenue: $2400/month.
changed positioning to "lead response automation that books 3x more sales calls." close rate: 24%. revenue: $8100/month.
the shift: stop talking about tools. start talking about outcomes. what does the automation actually do for their business.
how to position: identify the pain. quantify the cost. show the outcome. automation is just the how.
client pain: "im losing leads because i respond too slow." cost: "youre losing $15k/month in deals." outcome: "respond in 90sec, book 40% more calls."
now automation isnt a tech project. its a revenue solution.
this works for any service: dont sell seo. sell "rank for keywords that bring 200 leads per month." dont sell design. sell "landing page that converts 8% instead of 2%."
the arbitrage: you know how to build the automation. they dont. but you position it as a business outcome. premium pricing unlocked.
threads.cta: positioning templates + outcome calculator + pricing framework in the agency playbook. link in bio.

threads.hook: went from $0 to $10400 mrr in 90 days. no audience. no funding. just these 7 plays
tweets: went from $0 to $10400 mrr in 90 days. no audience. no funding. just these 7 plays
play 1: pick one money method. i chose app factory. dont spread thin. master one channel first.
play 2: clone a proven winner. found app making $100k/month. built niche version for faith market. shipped in 4 days.
play 3: aso optimization from day one. keyword research before building. title, subtitle, screenshots all optimized. 0.8% conversion vs industry 0.3%.
play 4: launch in simulator first. caught 12 bugs before submission. apple approved in 2 days instead of getting rejected.
play 5: repurpose all marketing content. one demo video became 23 pieces. tiktok, reels, youtube shorts, twitter. distribution on autopilot.
play 6: double down on what works. first app hit $800 mrr in 30 days. immediately built 3 more in adjacent niches.
play 7: automate everything repeatable. ralph loops for builds. remotion for videos. buffer for social. freed up 60% of my time to focus on strategy.
the timeline: week 1-2 (app 1 build + launch). week 3-4 (marketing + first revenue). week 5-8 (apps 2-4 build). week 9-12 (scale distribution).
revenue breakdown: app 1 ($2800 mrr). app 2 ($1900 mrr). app 3 ($3200 mrr). app 4 ($2500 mrr). total: $10400 mrr.
the constraint: had to stay under $200 monthly spend. so automation and claude code were critical. couldnt hire devs or agencies.
the lesson: you dont need a big audience. you dont need venture capital. you need one proven method and relentless execution.
threads.cta: full 90 day playbook with week by week tasks + templates in the starter kit. link in bio.
